summary: Surfaced the already-stamped GitStatus bundle field through BuildInfo, the Build Info sheet, and the diagnostic report so device/TestFlight provenance gates no longer have to infer clean/dirty state from commit suffixes or the Not releaseable banner. Added focused BuildInfoTests coverage for reading GitStatus from a stamped bundle and for the fallback value when the key is absent. Updated the release workflow wording to name Git status directly. Stamping behavior, releaseability rules, and TestFlight proof status were intentionally unchanged. A follow-up environment pass installed/selected a compatible iOS 26.5 simulator runtime and the runtime domain test now passes.
validations_passed: xcrun simctl runtime match set iphoneos26.5 23F77 --sdkBuild 23F73

contract_status_changes.before: Tools/generate-build-info.sh stamped GitStatus into bundle metadata, but BuildInfo did not read it and BuildInfoView did not show it directly. TestFlight/device provenance review had to infer clean/dirty state from GitCommit suffixes or the Not releaseable banner.
contract_status_changes.after: BuildInfo reads GitStatus with an unavailable fallback, diagnosticReport emits a stable 'Git status: ...' line, and BuildInfoView shows a dedicated Git status row in the Source section while preserving the existing Not releaseable banner and releaseability logic.

contract_status_changes.before: docs/workflows/release.md described dirty status generically and did not name the explicit stamped GitStatus field in the TestFlight diagnosis steps.
contract_status_changes.after: release.md now names GitStatus as stamped bundle metadata and tells operators to record Git status directly from Build Info during TestFlight diagnosis.

residual_risks: The runtime validation passed by selecting the new iOS 26.5 simulator explicitly via OWLORY_XCODE_DESTINATION='id=BE8450CB-77B6-4A56-81EA-9A1F95C22042'. The repo default destination still names iPhone 16 / iOS 26.3.1, so future agents on this machine should either use the selected 26.5 simulator override or update the default destination in a separate harness slice.

The iOS 26.5 runtime install first reported a duplicate disk-image record; CoreSimulator now has a ready 26.5 runtime and an unusable duplicate record. It did not affect the rerun, but future simulator cleanup may remove the duplicate via simctl runtime tooling if it becomes noisy.
BuildInfo.isReleaseable intentionally still follows the existing commit-suffix/missing-ref rule. This slice only displays GitStatus; it does not change release provenance policy.
BuildInfoView strings remain part of the existing Build Info support surface and were not localized in this diagnostics slice.
No running-app, screenshot, device, or TestFlight proof is claimed by this slice.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop after environment-compatible validations. The blocked TestFlight and localization slices remain parked behind their entry conditions.
